In cycle theory, one complete cycle is defined as from one low point (trough) to the next low point, and the theory uses the periodicity and position of peaks to understand the current cycle's conclusion and the beginning of the next cycle.\n\nCycle theory was proposed by American analyst Raymond A. Merriman and is based on the phenomenon that markets tend to bottom at regular intervals.\n\nThe search results indicate that this analytical method is applied to various markets including Bitcoin trading, with traders using it to attempt to predict price movements and identify trading opportunities.\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"6c0a3100-0d18-48ef-886e-caade7c7b33f","timestamp":"2026-04-03T18:25:48.998Z","sourceToolAssistantUUID":"1fb83c33-e4ba-4413-8d71-bd98dd288577","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/shuji/Desktop/kitt-voice","sessionId":"1f26b4f2-94cb-4562-b736-716e24d95f8e","version":"2.1.85","gitBranch":"main","slug":"foamy-gliding-lerdorf"}
